original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with established colorectal cancer and people receiving colorectal-cancer care; screening and undiagnosed symptoms require separate pathways.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports clinician-entered established-diagnosis, prior treatment, bowel-function impact, result, surveillance, recurrence, and follow-up documentation without interpreting colonoscopy or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.5 — information and treatment effects
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting treatment history, bowel, urinary, sexual, stoma, bleeding, discharge, functional, and patient-question context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.3 — ongoing care and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded CEA, CT, recurrence follow-up, stool frequency, urgency, continence, emptying, fragmentation, and functional impact.

title: Colorectal cancer — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults with established colorectal cancer and people receiving colorectal-cancer care; screening and undiagnosed symptoms require separate pathways.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
